description: The central command center for JARVIS — an autonomous agentic system. Claude always references this for major projects. Commander orchestrates all tools.
auto_trigger_rules: ANY PRD provided or referenced by the user triggers CLAUDE HQ activation — this means a long-running project
ANY mention of 'build me', 'create an app', 'develop a system' triggers full-stack activation
ANY multi-session project triggers token-efficiency at minimum
Claude should ALWAYS check this registry before starting a major project
ANY clone/install from an untrusted author → route through Trust Gate (TRUST_GATE.md)

tools.name: Autonomous Agent Development System
tools.description: Complete playbook for building software products autonomously with Claude. Micro-task decomposition, git-based context continuity, intelligent error handling. Result: working software in 60-240 minutes.
activation_triggers: Building a complete application or system from scratch
User says build me, create an app, develop a system
User provides or references a PRD
Complex project requiring 10+ tasks or components
Full-stack development with multiple layers
Projects requiring decomposition into 30-80 micro-tasks
anti_triggers: Single file edits or bug fixes
Documentation-only tasks
Quick code snippets or explanations
Tasks completable in under 10 minutes

setup_instructions.command: Fetch playbook from raw_playbook URL and follow initialization protocol

tools.integration_notes: Always pair with token-efficiency and claude-mem for large builds. Consider paul-framework for structured loop integrity.

tools.name: Token Efficiency and Context Continuity
tools.description: TECCP skill system: token discipline, context buffer monitoring, GitHub-backed task tracking, anti-hallucination framework, session lifecycle management.
activation_triggers: ANY multi-session project
ANY PRD provided
User mentions token efficiency or context continuity
Long-running conversations approaching context limits
User says pick up where we left off
anti_triggers: Single-turn factual questions
Quick conversations under one session

setup_instructions.command: Fetch SKILL.md from raw_skill URL and internalize as operating rules

tools.integration_notes: Foundational layer for ALL non-trivial sessions. Always active alongside any other tool.

tools.name: Claude-Mem Persistent Memory System
tools.description: Persistent memory compression system for Claude Code. Automatically captures tool usage observations, generates semantic summaries, and makes them available to future sessions. Includes MCP search tools, progressive disclosure, SQLite + Chroma vector database, and web viewer UI.
activation_triggers: Long-running projects spanning multiple sessions
Any PRD-driven project where context must persist
User says remember this or needs cross-session memory
Projects where decisions and observations must be recalled later
When token-efficiency CCD alone is insufficient — need richer persistent memory
Complex codebases where search over past observations adds value
anti_triggers: Single-session tasks
Quick one-off questions
Tasks where no persistent memory is needed
tools.integration_notes: Complements token-efficiency TECCP. TECCP handles within-session efficiency and CCD handoff; claude-mem handles persistent cross-session memory with semantic search. Use both for maximum continuity.

tools.name: RuFlo Enterprise AI Orchestration Platform
tools.description: Production-ready multi-agent AI orchestration for Claude Code. Deploy 60+ specialized agents in coordinated swarms with self-learning capabilities, fault-tolerant consensus, and enterprise-grade security. WASM kernels in Rust power the policy engine.
activation_triggers: Enterprise-scale projects requiring multiple coordinated agents
Projects needing parallel agent execution with swarm intelligence
Tasks requiring 60+ specialized agent types
When autonomous-agent-system needs more sophisticated multi-agent coordination
Projects requiring self-learning and self-optimizing agent architecture
User mentions multi-agent, swarm, or enterprise orchestration
anti_triggers: Simple single-agent tasks
Projects where autonomous-agent-system is sufficient
Quick builds under 2 hours
Non-Claude-Code environments

setup_instructions.alternative: Clone repo and follow README setup

tools.integration_notes: Heavy-duty alternative to autonomous-agent-system for enterprise-scale builds. Use when you need swarm coordination, not just sequential micro-tasks. Pairs well with claude-mem for persistent memory across agent swarms.

tools.name: Everything Claude Code — Performance Optimization System
tools.description: The performance optimization system for AI agent harnesses. 21 agents, 102 skills, 52 commands, hooks, rules, and MCP configs. Covers TDD, code review, security scanning, continuous learning, strategic compaction, multi-agent orchestration, and cross-platform support (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode).
activation_triggers: Need for structured development workflows (TDD, code review, security)
Want continuous learning — auto-extract patterns from sessions
Need code review agents, security scanning, or build error resolution
Projects requiring professional development practices
User mentions TDD, code review, security scan, or quality gates
When you want Claude Code to operate with production-grade development practices
Need AgentShield security auditing for Claude Code configs
anti_triggers: Pure prompt engineering or writing tasks
Non-coding tasks
Quick prototypes where quality gates would slow things down
tools.integration_notes: This is the quality and practices layer. Combines well with any build tool (autonomous-agent-system or ruflo) to add TDD, code review, security scanning. The continuous-learning-v2 system can auto-extract patterns that improve future sessions. Strategic compaction skill helps manage token usage.

tools.name: PAUL — Plan-Apply-Unify Loop
tools.description: Structured AI-assisted development framework for Claude Code. Enforces Plan-Apply-Unify loop integrity — every plan gets closed with mandatory UNIFY. Acceptance-driven development with BDD criteria, in-session context management, state persistence via STATE.md, and 26 slash commands.
activation_triggers: Projects requiring structured planning with acceptance criteria
When loop integrity matters — every plan must close properly
User wants BDD-style Given/When/Then acceptance criteria
Projects where state must persist across sessions via STATE.md
When autonomous-agent-system needs more structured planning gates
User mentions plan, acceptance criteria, loop, or structured development
Projects where quality and closure matter more than raw speed
anti_triggers: Quick prototypes or MVPs where speed beats structure
Single-file changes
Research or exploration tasks
Tasks where the overhead of PLAN/APPLY/UNIFY isn't justified

setup_instructions.post_install: Run /paul:init in Claude Code to initialize in your project

tools.integration_notes: PAUL and autonomous-agent-system solve similar problems differently. Autonomous-agent-system is speed-optimized (fire-and-forget micro-tasks). PAUL is quality-optimized (mandatory closure, acceptance criteria). Choose based on project needs, or combine: use PAUL for planning/closure and autonomous-agent-system for execution. SEED is the ideation phase that feeds into PAUL — always consider SEED first when starting a new project.

tools.name: SEED — Typed Project Incubator
tools.description: AI project incubator that takes raw ideas through type-aware guided exploration and produces structured PLANNING.md files ready for building. 5 project types (Application/deep, Workflow/standard, Client/standard, Utility/tight, Campaign/creative) each with adaptive rigor, guided conversation sections, and type-specific templates. Coach persona brainstorms alongside you — not an interrogator. Graduates mature plans into buildable project directories. /seed launch does graduation + headless PAUL init in one command — zero-friction ideation-to-build handoff. Pure markdown, zero runtime deps.
activation_triggers: User has a new project idea and needs to plan it out before building
User says I have an idea, I want to plan, let me think this through, or seed this
ANY new project that will use PAUL — SEED should run first to produce PLANNING.md
User wants guided ideation or brainstorming for an app, workflow, client site, utility, or campaign
User mentions campaign planning, content campaign, or marketing launch planning
User is starting a project from scratch and hasn't defined requirements yet
User provides a vague idea that needs structured exploration before building
User says /seed or /seed launch
anti_triggers: User already has a detailed PRD or PLANNING.md ready
Quick fixes, bug fixes, or single-file changes
Projects already in progress with PAUL
Tasks where requirements are already crystal clear

setup_instructions.post_install: Run /seed in Claude Code to start guided ideation

tools.integration_notes: SEED is the ideation phase that precedes PAUL. The workflow is: SEED (ideate → PLANNING.md) → PAUL (structured build from PLANNING.md). /seed launch wraps both: graduates the plan and initializes PAUL with headless context so PAUL never re-asks questions SEED already answered. For campaign-type projects, pairs naturally with claude-creator-skills and humanizer. Part of the Chris Kahler ecosystem (PAUL, AEGIS, BASE, CARL, Skillsmith).

tools.name: Recall Stack — 5-Layer Memory Architecture
tools.description: 5-layer persistent memory system for Claude Code. Layer 1: CLAUDE.md rules. Layer 2: primer.md auto-rewriting session state (survives crashes). Layer 3: Git context injection via SessionStart hook. Layer 4: Hindsight behavioral learning (learns from corrections across sessions via Docker). Layer 5: Obsidian vault as mounted knowledge base.
activation_triggers: ANY multi-session project where session state must survive crashes or restarts
User wants Claude to remember what was done last session and pick up seamlessly
Projects where behavioral learning from past corrections is valuable
User mentions recall, session memory, primer, or pick up where we left off
Need for automatic git context injection at every session start
User wants Obsidian vault integrated as knowledge base context
When claude-mem alone is insufficient — need structured layered memory with auto-rewriting state
anti_triggers: Single-session tasks with no continuity needs
Quick one-off questions or fixes
Projects where existing CLAUDE.md + claude-mem coverage is sufficient
tools.integration_notes: Complements both claude-mem and token-efficiency. claude-mem handles semantic search over past observations. token-efficiency handles within-session token discipline. recall-stack adds structured layered memory: auto-rewriting primer.md for crash-safe session state, git context injection, Hindsight behavioral learning, and Obsidian knowledge base. For maximum memory coverage, use all three together. Layers 1-3 and 5 work without Docker; Layer 4 (Hindsight) gracefully degrades if Docker is unavailable.

tools.name: Claude Creator Skills — 8-Skill Content Creation System
tools.description: Complete closed-loop content creator system with 8 specialized skills: audience research, brand voice guardian, content ideation, killer scripter, visual storyteller, content repurposer, offer/bio writer, and performance debriefer. Skills form a pipeline from research to measurement.

audience-research-analyst.purpose: Mine audience comments/DMs/forums to extract real pain points, exact language, and content ideas
triggers: find content ideas from real people

brand-voice-guardian.purpose: Extract and protect the creator's authentic voice across all content — builds a reusable Voice Card
triggers: make it sound more like me

content-ideation.purpose: Generate 15-25 strategic content ideas from audience pain, personal story, and niche gaps
triggers: I don't know what to post

killer-scripter.purpose: Write scripts for Reels, YouTube, carousels that feel like conversation — 50 hook templates included

visual-storyteller.purpose: Turn scripts into shot-by-shot visual plans with art direction and AI image/video prompts

content-repurposer.purpose: Turn one long-form piece into 10-15 platform-native pieces across Instagram, YouTube, X, LinkedIn, email

offer-and-bio-writer.purpose: Write bios, link-in-bio pages, landing copy, and DM scripts that convert attention into action

content-performance-debriefer.purpose: Analyze content metrics to reveal what worked, why, and what to do next — weekly debriefs and monthly patterns

activation_triggers: User wants to create content (scripts, reels, carousels, posts, videos)
User mentions audience research, brand voice, content ideas, or content strategy
User says write a script, write a reel, plan my content, or repurpose this
User provides analytics screenshots or asks about content performance
User wants to build or refine their personal brand or creator business
User mentions bio, landing page, offer copy, or DM scripts
User asks for visual direction, shot lists, or AI image/video prompts
ANY content creation workflow — from ideation through measurement
anti_triggers: Pure software development tasks with no content creation
Non-creator business tasks
Tasks where no content writing or strategy is needed

setup_instructions.command: Read the relevant .skill file(s) from ~/claude-hq/tools/claude-creator-skills/ and internalize as operating behavior for this session
setup_instructions.skill_selection_guide: Match the user's intent to the skills_index triggers. Load one or more skills as needed. For a full content session, load them in pipeline order: research → voice → ideation → script → visual → repurpose → offer → debrief.

tools.integration_notes: First-party skill collection stored directly in the CLAUDE HQ repo (not an external reference). Each .skill file is self-contained with activation rules, output formats, and behavioral instructions. Load individual skills based on task, or chain multiple skills for a full content pipeline. Pairs well with brand-voice-guardian as a persistent filter across all other creator skills.

tools.name: Agent Skills Mega Collection — 256 Skills
tools.description: Massive 256-skill collection with skills_index.json for lookup. Covers AI/ML agents (63), DevOps/infrastructure (47), security/pentesting (36), marketing/SEO (34), web development (29), design/UI (17), game development (10), APIs, databases, and more. Each skill is a directory under skills/skills/[name]/ with instruction files.
activation_triggers: User needs a specialized skill not covered by other registered tools
User mentions pentesting, security audit, SEO, game dev, or niche topics
User says find me a skill for X or I need a skill for Y
Broad development tasks where a specialized skill would improve output quality
User mentions any topic in the 256-skill index (AI agents, marketing, infrastructure, etc.)
anti_triggers: Tasks already covered by a more specific registered tool
Simple tasks that don't need specialized skill instructions

setup_instructions.command: Search ~/claude-hq/tools/agent-skills-mega/skills_index.json for matching skill, then read from ~/claude-hq/repos/agent-skills-mega/skills/skills/[name]/
setup_instructions.skill_selection_guide: Parse skills_index.json to find skills matching the user's intent by name or description. Read the skill's directory for instruction files.

tools.integration_notes: This is the catch-all skill library. If no other registered tool has a matching skill, search this index. The skills_index.json provides id, name, description, and path for all 256 skills. First-party — stored directly in CLAUDE HQ repo.

tools.name: Awesome Claude Code Subagents — 138 Specialized Agents
tools.description: 138 specialized Claude Code subagent definitions across 10 categories: core development, language specialists (27), infrastructure (15), quality/security (15), data/AI (13), developer experience (14), specialized domains (13), business/product (12), meta-orchestration (11), and research/analysis (7).
activation_triggers: User needs specialized subagents for Claude Code tasks
Projects requiring language-specific expertise (Rust, Go, Python, etc.)
Need for specialized domain agents (fintech, healthcare, IoT, etc.)
Quality/security auditing subagents needed
Meta-orchestration — coordinating multiple subagents
User mentions subagent, specialized agent, or language expert
anti_triggers: Simple tasks that don't need specialized agents
Non-Claude-Code environments

setup_instructions.usage: Browse categories/ directory for relevant agent definitions, read and apply them

tools.integration_notes: Complements everything-claude-code (which provides skills/commands/hooks) by adding 138 dedicated subagent definitions. Use when you need a specialized agent role rather than a skill or workflow.

tools.name: Remotion — Programmatic Video with React
tools.description: Official Remotion skill for Claude Code. Programmatic video creation using React — compositions, animations, audio sync, rendering to MP4/GIF. Build videos as code.
activation_triggers: User wants to create videos programmatically with React
User mentions Remotion, programmatic video, or video-as-code
Projects needing automated video generation or animation rendering
User says create a video, animate this, or render video
anti_triggers: Manual video editing tasks
Non-React video projects
Simple image or static content creation

setup_instructions.usage: Read skills/remotion/ directory for Remotion best practices and patterns

tools.integration_notes: Pairs well with visual-storyteller creator skill for a complete pipeline: visual-storyteller plans the shots, Remotion renders them programmatically.

tools.name: Google Stitch — Design-to-Code Skills
tools.description: Google Labs Stitch skills for Claude Code. 3 skills: design-md (generate design system docs from code), react-components (generate components from design specs), stitch-loop (iterative design-to-code refinement workflow).
activation_triggers: User wants to generate React components from design specs
User mentions design system, design-to-code, or Stitch
Projects needing automated design documentation
Iterative UI refinement workflows
User says turn this design into code or generate components from this design
anti_triggers: Non-React projects
Backend-only tasks
Tasks with no design/UI component

setup_instructions.usage: Read skills/ directory — design-md, react-components, stitch-loop

tools.integration_notes: Useful for frontend-heavy projects. Pairs with everything-claude-code for full dev practices. The stitch-loop skill provides an iterative refinement workflow that complements PAUL framework's Plan-Apply-Unify loop.

tools.name: Humanizer — AI Text Humanization Skill
tools.description: Claude Code skill that removes 25 signs of AI-generated writing to make text sound natural and human. Based on Wikipedia's 'Signs of AI writing' guide (WikiProject AI Cleanup). Detects content patterns (significance inflation, promotional language, vague attributions), language patterns (AI vocabulary, copula avoidance, rule of three), style patterns (em dash/boldface/emoji overuse), communication patterns (chatbot artifacts, sycophantic tone), and filler/hedging. Includes two-pass audit: draft rewrite → 'what still looks AI?' → final revision. v2.3.0.
activation_triggers: User asks to humanize text or make AI text sound human
User says this sounds like AI or make it sound more natural
User is writing marketing copy, campaign text, or brand content
User asks to rewrite or edit text to remove AI patterns
User mentions AI detection, AI writing patterns, or sounding robotic
ANY content creation task where the output must pass as human-written
User says humanize this, make this human, or remove AI patterns
Content destined for social media, landing pages, emails, or campaigns
anti_triggers: Code generation or technical documentation
Tasks where AI-sounding output is acceptable
Internal notes or drafts not meant for publication

setup_instructions.command: Read ~/claude-hq/tools/humanizer/SKILL.md and internalize as operating behavior for this session
setup_instructions.skill_selection_guide: Load when any text output needs to sound human-written. Especially important for marketing copy, social posts, campaign content, landing pages, and any public-facing writing.

tools.integration_notes: Critical companion to claude-creator-skills. Load humanizer as a post-processing filter after killer-scripter, content-repurposer, offer-and-bio-writer, or any skill that generates public-facing text. Also useful standalone for editing any AI-generated content. The two-pass audit (draft → AI detection → final revision) ensures thorough humanization.

tools.name: Code Review Graph — Token-Efficient Codebase Knowledge Graph
tools.description: Local knowledge graph for Claude Code. Tree-sitter parsing across 19 languages, SQLite-backed. 6.8x fewer tokens on code reviews, up to 49x on daily coding tasks. Incremental updates in under 2 seconds. MCP tools for precise context injection.
activation_triggers: ANY coding project — this should be always active
Code reviews or pull request analysis
Navigating unfamiliar codebases
Any task where Claude would normally read entire source files
Large monorepos where token waste is most painful
anti_triggers: Non-coding tasks (content, research, design-only)
Projects with fewer than 5 files

cost_profile.notes: Open source, local SQLite, zero cloud calls

tools.integration_notes: Foundational token saver. Always pair with TECCP. code-review-graph handles codebase-level efficiency (which files to read), TECCP handles session-level efficiency (how to respond). Together they can reduce token usage by 80%+.

tools.name: Graphify — Multimodal Knowledge Graph Builder
tools.description: Turn any folder of code, docs, PDFs, images, videos into a queryable knowledge graph. 71.5x fewer tokens per query. Native Obsidian vault export. Tree-sitter AST for 20 languages plus LLM semantic extraction. Leiden clustering, god node detection, community analysis.
activation_triggers: Understanding a new codebase or project
Building a knowledge base from mixed content (code + docs + papers + images)
User says /graphify or wants to map a project
Need to export project knowledge to Obsidian
Research tasks requiring structured knowledge from multiple sources
Any Karpathy-style /raw folder with mixed content
anti_triggers: Simple single-file tasks
Tasks where code-review-graph alone is sufficient (pure code, no docs)

cost_profile.notes: Open source. Uses Claude API for semantic extraction of non-code files only.

tools.integration_notes: The bridge between your codebase and Obsidian. Use --obsidian flag to auto-generate vaults. Complements code-review-graph: CRG handles code structure, graphify handles broader knowledge (docs, research, multimodal). Together with recall-stack L5 (Obsidian), forms the complete second brain.

tools.name: MemPalace — Verbatim AI Memory System
tools.description: Stores conversations verbatim in ChromaDB without summarisation. Palace architecture (wings/halls/rooms) for structured retrieval. 170 tokens on startup, semantic search on demand. 19 MCP tools. Runs fully offline. AAAK compression for token efficiency.
activation_triggers: Long-running projects where every decision must be recallable
When claude-mem summaries lose critical context
Cross-project memory where decisions in project A affect project B
User says remember everything or needs verbatim recall
anti_triggers: Single-session tasks
Tasks where claude-mem coverage is sufficient
tools.integration_notes: Complements claude-mem. claude-mem captures compressed observations; MemPalace stores raw verbatim conversations. Use claude-mem for quick recall, MemPalace for deep recall when exact wording matters.

tools.name: Lightpanda — Headless Browser for AI Agents
tools.description: Headless browser built from scratch in Zig for AI agents. 9x faster, 16x less memory than Chrome. CDP compatible with Playwright/Puppeteer. No visual rendering — optimised for data extraction, scraping, and DOM manipulation.
activation_triggers: Web scraping or data extraction tasks
Headless browsing where visual rendering is not needed
High-volume page fetching (speed and memory matter)
AI agent browsing workflows
anti_triggers: Tasks requiring screenshots or visual testing
Complex SPAs that need full Chrome rendering
Tasks requiring visual interaction — use Playwright MCP instead
tools.integration_notes: Use Lightpanda for data extraction tasks (scraping, crawling). Use Playwright MCP for interactive tasks (screenshots, form filling, visual testing). Commander chooses based on whether the task needs to 'see' or just 'read'.

tools.name: Playwright MCP — Browser Automation with Visual Rendering
tools.description: Official Playwright MCP server for full browser automation. Screenshots, form filling, visual testing, interaction with complex web apps. Uses Chromium for full rendering.
activation_triggers: Tasks requiring visual browser interaction
Screenshot capture or visual testing
Form filling or UI interaction
Testing web applications visually
Tasks where Lightpanda's no-render limitation is a problem
anti_triggers: Pure data extraction or scraping — use Lightpanda instead (faster)
Tasks that don't need visual rendering
tools.integration_notes: Pair with Lightpanda for a complete browser strategy. Lightpanda handles speed-critical data extraction; Playwright handles visual interaction. For subagent isolation, use --isolated flag.

tools.name: Nano Banana 2 — AI Image Generation CLI
tools.description: AI image generation CLI powered by Gemini. Green screen transparency, reference images, style transfer, aspect ratios. ~$0.04/image. Also a Claude Code plugin. Tracks cost per generation.
activation_triggers: Generate images for websites, apps, or content
Create transparent assets (icons, logos, mascots)
UI design iterations — quickly generate design variations
Thumbnails, social media graphics, hero images
Style transfer or image editing
User says generate an image or create a visual
anti_triggers: Tasks with no visual component
When user explicitly wants a different image gen service

setup_instructions.command: git clone https://github.com/kingbootoshi/nano-banana-2-skill.git ~/tools/nano-banana-2 && cd ~/tools/nano-banana-2 && bun install && bun link
requirements: Gemini API key (free tier available at ai.google.dev)

tools.integration_notes: Fills the visual asset gap. Pairs with visual-storyteller (plans the shots) and SuperDesign (UI mockups). nano-banana generates the actual images. Cost tracking built in via ~/.nano-banana/costs.json.

tools.name: UI/UX Pro Max — Design Intelligence Skill
tools.description: Design intelligence with 50+ styles, 161 colour palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types, 99 UX guidelines, 25 chart types across 10 technology stacks. AI-powered Design System Generator. BM25 search engine for recommendations.
activation_triggers: ANY frontend or UI design task
Building websites, landing pages, dashboards, apps
Choosing colour schemes, typography, layout systems
Reviewing UI code for UX, accessibility, visual consistency
User mentions design, UI, UX, landing page, dashboard
anti_triggers: Backend-only tasks with no UI
Pure API or data pipeline work

setup_instructions.alternative: npm install -g uipro-cli && uipro init --ai claude

tools.integration_notes: The design brain — makes design decisions (style, colours, typography). Pair with SuperDesign (generates mockups) and emilkowalski/skill (animation quality). Pipeline: ui-ux-pro-max decides → SuperDesign visualises → emilkowalski polishes.

tools.name: Emil Kowalski Design Engineering Skill
tools.description: Design engineering skill covering animations, design, code, and performance. Spring physics, easing functions, gesture handling, Framer Motion/Motion best practices. Based on Emil Kowalski's articles and course.
activation_triggers: Implementing animations or transitions in React/web
Reviewing animation quality
Hover effects, modals, carousels, page transitions
User mentions animation, motion, transition, spring, easing
anti_triggers: Non-frontend tasks
Static content with no interactive elements
tools.integration_notes: The animation quality layer. Use case-by-case, not always-on. Activate when frontend agent is building interactive UI. Pairs with ui-ux-pro-max (design decisions) and stitch-skills (design-to-code).

tools.name: SuperDesign — AI Design Agent + MCP Server
tools.description: Open-source AI design agent that generates UI mockups, wireframes, and production-ready components from natural language. Generates 10+ design variants in parallel. Runs locally, MIT licensed. MCP server available for Claude Code.
activation_triggers: Generating UI mockups or wireframes before coding
Exploring multiple design directions quickly
User wants to see design options before committing
Prototyping new pages or components
User says show me design options or mockup
anti_triggers: Already have a design/mockup to implement
Backend-only work
Tasks where design exploration isn't needed

cost_profile.notes: Open source, runs locally, MIT licensed

setup_instructions.command: Clone https://github.com/jonthebeef/superdesign-mcp-claude-code and configure as MCP server
setup_instructions.alternative: Install VS Code extension from marketplace

tools.integration_notes: Visual prototyping layer. Use before coding: SuperDesign generates mockup variants → user picks one → frontend agent implements it. Works best in the pipeline: ui-ux-pro-max (design system) → SuperDesign (mockups) → emilkowalski/skill (animations) → code.

tools.name: OpenMontage — Agentic Video Production System
tools.description: Open-source agentic video production with 11 pipelines, 49 tools, 400+ agent skills. Explainers, talking heads, screen demos, cinematic trailers, animations, podcasts, localisation. Uses Remotion for composition. Quality enforcement via contract validation.
activation_triggers: Video production of any kind
Explainer videos, product demos, trailers
Content repurposing into video format
Podcast editing or clip extraction
User mentions video, explainer, talking head, trailer, animation
anti_triggers: Tasks with no video component
Simple image generation — use nano-banana-2 instead
Audio-only tasks

tools.integration_notes: Full video production pipeline. Pairs naturally with creator skills: killer-scripter (writes script) → visual-storyteller (plans shots) → OpenMontage (produces video). Also uses Remotion (already registered) for composition.

tools.name: Wan2GP — Local AI Video Generator (GPU Required)
tools.description: AI video generator optimised for consumer NVIDIA GPUs. Supports Wan 2.1/2.2, Hunyuan Video, LTX Video, Flux. Has internal API for agent integration. Includes Deepy agent for offline operation.
activation_triggers: Generating AI video clips locally
Text-to-video or image-to-video generation
Only when NVIDIA GPU is available
anti_triggers: No NVIDIA GPU available (Mac Apple Silicon)
Tasks where stock footage or screen recording suffice
Simple editing tasks — use OpenMontage instead

cost_profile.notes: Open source, runs locally. Requires NVIDIA GPU with sufficient VRAM.

setup_instructions.command: git clone https://github.com/deepbeepmeep/Wan2GP.git && conda create -n wan2gp python=3.10.9

tools.integration_notes: Backend video gen engine for OpenMontage when AI-generated clips are needed. CONDITIONAL — only register and activate if NVIDIA GPU hardware is confirmed available.

tools.name: Agent Forge — Precision Agent Factory
tools.description: Meta-skill that produces precision-crafted autonomous agents using prompt-engineer methodology. 5-phase forge protocol: Define → Architect → Stress-Test → Produce → File. Outputs SKILL.md files into the Agent Bank with registry entries for Commander auto-discovery.
activation_triggers: User says forge an agent, create an agent, build me an agent for X
Commander needs a specialized agent that doesn't exist in the agent bank
User says new agent, agent forge, or I need an agent that...
Building a suite of agents for a multi-agent workflow
anti_triggers: Using an existing agent from the bank — just load it, don't re-forge
Quick one-off tasks that don't need a reusable agent
Modifying an existing agent — update its SKILL.md directly

setup_instructions.command: Read ~/Claude_Skills/agent-forge/SKILL.md and ~/Claude_Skills/prompt-engineer/SKILL.md — both must be loaded for the forge to operate

tools.integration_notes: The agent factory. Depends on prompt-engineer for methodology. Outputs to ~/claude-hq/agents/ with registry entries. Commander scans the agent registry at task classification time to find matching agents. The forge only runs when a NEW agent is needed — existing agents are loaded directly.

tools.name: Agent Bank — Precision Agent Registry
tools.description: Registry of precision-crafted agents forged via Agent Forge. Commander reads ~/claude-hq/agents/registry.json at task classification time to find matching agents by activation triggers. Each agent is a self-contained SKILL.md.
activation_triggers: ANY task that might have a matching specialized agent
Commander Step 2 (task classification) — always scan agent bank
User says use my agents, check the agent bank, or which agents do I have
anti_triggers: Creating new agents — that's agent-forge, not agent-bank

setup_instructions.command: Read ~/claude-hq/agents/registry.json and scan agents array for matching activation_triggers

tools.integration_notes: Passive registry — Commander consults it during task classification. No setup needed. Agents are added by agent-forge. To use an agent: read its SKILL.md from the path in the registry entry.

tools.name: skills.sh — The Open Agent Skills Ecosystem
tools.description: Marketplace of ~91,000 agent skills across 19+ AI coding agents. Skills are GitHub-packaged, searchable via `npx skills find`, installed via `npx skills add <owner/repo@skill>`. Known threat ecosystem: Snyk Feb 2026 found 13.4% of skills have critical issues; never install without the Trust Gate.
activation_triggers: No matching tool in registry.json for the current task
User explicitly asks to search skills.sh
Commander Step 2.5 — skill discovery fallback
anti_triggers: Registry already has a matching tool (use it first)
Author is in INCIDENT_LEDGER cooling-off (blocked)
User invokes `npx skills add` directly (blocked by PreToolUse hook — route through /scout)

setup_instructions.command: npx skills find <query> (no install — use /scout for gated install)

tools.integration_notes: Access ONLY through the /scout slash command. The Trust Gate PreToolUse hook blocks raw `npx skills add`. Skills.sh is itself owned by vercel-labs — see INCIDENT_LEDGER.md for cooling-off status. Tier C pipeline mandatory for every install regardless of install count.

tools.name: Magika — AI-Powered File Type Detection
tools.description: Google's deep-learning file-type classifier. 99% accuracy, ~5ms per file, 200+ file types, Apache 2.0. Powers Layer 1 of the Trust Gate — catches extension-spoofed payloads in any external code. Used at Google for hundred-billions-of-files-per-week scale in Gmail/Drive/Safe Browsing.
activation_triggers: Any external code clone or install (ambient via PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks)
Historical audit via /skill-audit
Explicit `scripts/trust-gate-post.sh <dir>` for out-of-session clones
anti_triggers: Already-trusted files on the local filesystem you authored
Content-sensitive files where type is already known
tools.integration_notes: Layer 1 of the Trust Gate. Not a standalone security tool — paired with secret-scan (Layer 2), Socket (Layer 3), and reputation (Layer 4) for defence-in-depth. See commander/TRUST_GATE.md.

tools.name: Socket CLI — Malicious Package Behaviour Detection
tools.description: Behavioural scanner for npm packages — catches malicious behaviour (typosquatting, compromised maintainers, obfuscated code, unexpected network/shell activity). Free tier. Surfaced the Axios 4.2.1 compromise within 6 minutes of registry publish. Complements CVE scanners, does not replace them.
activation_triggers: Tier C pipeline for any skill with package.json
Ambient Tier B when cloning anything with npm dependencies
anti_triggers: Python-only projects (use pip-audit instead)
Already-installed global packages (use `socket scan ~/.npm` if needed)
tools.integration_notes: Layer 3 of the Trust Gate for npm. Paired with pip-audit for Python. Failure is fail-closed — if Socket scan errors, treat as block for safety.

tools.name: pip-audit — Python Dependency CVE Scanner
tools.description: PyPA's official audit tool. Scans requirements.txt / pyproject.toml against the Python Packaging Advisory Database and OSV. Catches known CVEs in Python dependencies — complements Socket (which catches malicious behaviour) by covering the CVE layer.
activation_triggers: Tier C pipeline for any skill with requirements.txt or pyproject.toml
Any Python project being cloned or audited
anti_triggers: Non-Python projects
tools.integration_notes: Layer 3 of the Trust Gate for Python. Paired with Socket for npm. CVE-based, so catches known vulns but not novel maliciousness (that's Socket's role).

tools.name: Trail of Bits — Claude Code Security Skills
tools.description: Gold-standard security-focused Claude Code skills from Trail of Bits — one of the most respected security research firms. Covers security research, vulnerability detection, and audit workflows. Reference implementation for high-trust skill authoring.
activation_triggers: User requests security review, vulnerability scan, or threat modelling
Commander classifies task as security-sensitive
Reference implementation lookup for skill authoring
anti_triggers: Non-security tasks where generic skills suffice
tools.integration_notes: Trusted by allowlist. Still runs through full file-content scan on install. First repo installed via the Trust Gate at HQ v2 rollout — validates pipeline on known-good input.

tools.name: API Index — Free Public APIs Discovery Tool
tools.description: Searchable index of 3,932 free public APIs combining public-apis/public-apis (1,443 markdown entries) and APIs.guru (2,489 entries with OpenAPI schemas). Three search modes: FTS5 keyword (default, fastest), pure semantic via local fastembed embeddings (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5), and hybrid (FTS retrieval + embedding rerank — best quality). Includes auto-generation of typed client libraries from OpenAPI schemas via openapi-generator. MCP server registered as 'api-index' exposes 7 tools to Claude Code agents.
activation_triggers: User asks: is there a free API for X?
Project kickoff that needs an external data source — discover before paying or scraping
User says: find me an API for, what data sources exist for, free APIs for
Any task involving fetching real-world data (FX rates, weather, crypto, news, geocoding, government, music, etc.)
Need a typed client library for a known API — use generate-client subcommand
Commander Step 4 (credential planning) — check if a free alternative API exists before recommending paid SaaS
anti_triggers: Scraping arbitrary websites — APIs only cover sites that publish official endpoints; use Firecrawl/Playwright/Lightpanda for scraping
Runtime auto-call of APIs by an agent — this tool is for *discovery*, not *execution* (auth sprawl, ToS risk per LESSONS R1)
Pre-generating wrappers for all 2,489 OpenAPI entries — only generate on demand for ones actually used
Replacement for Firecrawl, Exa, or Reddit MCP — those are the right primitives for fetching, this is for finding
dependencies: mcp (pip, optional — only for MCP server)

setup_instructions.command: cd ~/claude-hq/tools/api-index && python3 scripts/build_index.py && python3 scripts/build_embeddings.py. Global symlink: ln -sf $(pwd)/bin/api-index ~/.local/bin/api-index. MCP server registered in ~/.claude.json mcpServers as 'api-index' — restart Claude Code to activate.

tools.integration_notes: Use at project kickoff before recommending paid SaaS or scraping. CLI supports --semantic for vague queries (e.g. 'real-time market sentiment' matches stock+news+sentiment APIs without keyword overlap), --hybrid for best overall quality. After Claude Code restart, agents can call search_apis / semantic_search_apis / hybrid_search_apis / get_api / list_categories / apis_by_category / index_stats directly via MCP. Quarterly refresh: api-index refresh.
tools.model_routing: haiku — discovery is mechanical, no architecture decisions needed
tools.cost: $0 — local SQLite + ONNX embeddings, no API keys
tools.reliability: high — deterministic FTS5 search; semantic mode produces consistent rankings (model is local)

tools.name: Crawl4AI — LLM-ready web scraping (native, no Docker)
tools.description: Self-hosted web crawler that returns LLM-ready markdown from any URL, including JS-heavy SPAs and dynamic content. Same source as the Crawl4AI Docker image, but installed as a native Python package in a dedicated venv — no Docker daemon required.
activation_triggers: Fetching JS-heavy SPAs where Jina Reader returns degraded content
Anti-bot pages requiring Playwright-quality fetch + clean markdown
Bulk research scrapes where output cleanliness matters more than per-fetch latency
Replaces Firecrawl (which is trial-free only, $16/mo recurring)
anti_triggers: Routine static pages — Jina Reader is faster and zero-setup
Pages requiring screenshots — use Playwright MCP
When the URL is trivially static and Jina suffices

cost_profile.notes: Open source, runs locally in dedicated venv

setup_instructions.trust_gate_note: Author 'unclecode' not currently allowlisted. Manually verified: Apache 2.0, 65k GitHub stars, active March 2026 release. HQ_TRUST_OVERRIDE used once for the install; logged.

wrapper.output_format: JSON envelope: {url, status, source, content, duration_ms}

tools.integration_notes: Crawl4AI is the heavy-quality fetch path. The fetch-page.sh wrapper tries Jina Reader first by default (mode=quick) and only invokes Crawl4AI when --mode auto detects degraded content or when --mode deep forces it. Layer 0 primitive — every research-heavy project (Corporate Brains, Labuan, NFL Spain, Watchdog telemetry investigations) uses it on tap.

tools.name: Jina Reader — hosted URL→markdown service
tools.description: Hosted service that returns clean markdown for any URL. Just prepend https://r.jina.ai/ to the URL. ~80% the quality of Crawl4AI/Firecrawl on JS-heavy sites; on par for static pages. Zero setup, no daemon, no container.
activation_triggers: Routine page fetches where speed + zero-setup matter
Static blogs, news, documentation pages
First attempt in fetch-page.sh's auto mode (escalates to Crawl4AI on degraded content)
anti_triggers: JS-heavy SPAs (use Crawl4AI)
Anti-bot / Cloudflare-challenged pages (use Crawl4AI)

cost_profile.notes: 20 req/min without key, 500 req/min with free key (set JINA_API_KEY env var)

commands: # No install. Optional: get a free key at https://jina.ai/ and export JINA_API_KEY

wrapper.output_format: JSON envelope: {url, status, source, content, duration_ms}

tools.integration_notes: Default workhorse. ~4-6× faster than Crawl4AI for routine pages. Layer 0 alongside crawl4ai-native; together they replace Firecrawl entirely.

tool_combinations.name: PRD Full Build Stack
tool_combinations.description: TRIGGERED AUTOMATICALLY when user provides a PRD. Complete toolchain for production application builds.
activation_order: 2. Activate recall-stack (crash-safe session state + git context + behavioral learning)
4. Load everything-claude-code practices (TDD, code review, security)
8. Begin autonomous execution with quality gates

tool_combinations.name: Full Project Build Stack
tool_combinations.description: For building complete applications without a formal PRD.

tool_combinations.name: SEED → PAUL Ideation-to-Build Pipeline
tool_combinations.description: Complete idea-to-build pipeline. SEED guides ideation and produces a structured PLANNING.md, then PAUL picks it up for managed build execution. Use when starting a new project from an idea.
trigger_patterns: Let me think this through then build
activation_order: 5. Run /seed launch — graduates plan + headless PAUL init
7. Begin PAUL Plan-Apply-Unify loop from PLANNING.md

tool_combinations.name: Structured Quality Build Stack
tool_combinations.description: When quality and closure matter more than speed. Uses PAUL for loop integrity. Consider running SEED first if requirements aren't fully defined.
activation_order: 4. (Optional) Run SEED first if requirements unclear — /seed produces PLANNING.md
7. Run /paul:init (reads PLANNING.md if SEED was used)

tool_combinations.name: Enterprise Swarm Build Stack
tool_combinations.description: For enterprise-scale projects requiring coordinated multi-agent swarms.
activation_order: 2. Activate recall-stack (session state + behavioral learning across swarm sessions)

tool_combinations.name: Creator Content Pipeline Stack
tool_combinations.description: Full content creation workflow from audience research to performance measurement. Activates relevant creator skills based on where the user is in the pipeline. Humanizer runs as a post-processing filter on all public-facing text.
activation_order: 1. Identify where user is in the creator pipeline
2. Load brand-voice-guardian if voice card exists (persistent filter)
3. Load the specific skill(s) matching the user's intent
4. For full pipeline: research → voice → ideation → script → visual → repurpose → offer → debrief
5. Run humanizer as post-processing filter on all public-facing text output
6. Deliver action-ready output in the skill's defined format

tool_combinations.name: Web Design Stack
tool_combinations.description: Complete web design pipeline from design system to polished, animated frontend.
activation_order: 4. Run SuperDesign to generate mockup variants
8. Use nano-banana-2 for hero images, icons, assets

tool_combinations.name: Video Production Stack
tool_combinations.description: Full video production from script to finished video.
activation_order: 2. Run humanizer on script (if narration will be public)

tool_combinations.name: Research & Analysis Stack
tool_combinations.description: Deep research with knowledge graph output and Obsidian integration.
activation_order: 2. Use Lightpanda for web scraping/data collection
3. Run graphify on collected materials (--obsidian for vault export)
4. Query the knowledge graph for insights
5. Store findings in recall-stack and claude-mem

tool_combinations.name: Full Creative Brief Stack
tool_combinations.description: Complete creative campaign from audience research through video production and performance measurement.
activation_order: 3. Use Lightpanda + graphify for competitive research
5. Load killer-scripter (write scripts for selected ideas)
11. Run humanizer on ALL public-facing text
